titlestmt.titleproper: Miss Rowe collection of botanical specimens

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

address.addressline: Department of Rare Books and Manuscripts

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 21:03

did.unittitle: Miss Rowe collection of botanical specimens

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

did.abstract: The collection comprises over 500 dried flower specimens from the area around Liverpool, assembled by Miss Rowe, of 2 Elizabeth Street, in 1861.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises over 500 dried flower specimens from the area around Liverpool, assembled by Miss Rowe, of 2 Elizabeth Street, in 1861. Each specimen is mounted on a folded sheet of paper and held in place by small bands of paper. On the facing page of the folded sheet, Rowe identifies the specimen with its Latin and common name, the location the specimen was found, and the date collected. Each housed within a blue tinted envelope (13 x 20 cm), some of which are still sealed. The front of each envelope is adorned by a watercolor of the flower enclosed, along with a printed label (presumably manufactured for collecting activities such as this) giving the Latin name of family to which the specimens belong.

The collection is accompanied by the original large mahogany box (52 x 35 x 10 cm) used to house the envelopes; with a hinged lid, brass lock, and key. On the inside of the lid is a paper label reading "L.N.F.C. Names of natural orders from Dr. Dickinson's Flora of Liverpool." Dickinson's work was published in 1851, and in 1914 his own herbarium was given to the university.
Also present: a large folder with a quantity of dried specimens; engraved name card bearing the hand-written address of Miss Rowe, with a watercolor forget-me-not on the envelope.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The collection is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Curator of Rare Books and Manuscripts.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open without restriction.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Miss Rowe Collection of Botanical Specimens, Yale Center for British Art, Paul Mellon Fund

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Yale Center for British Art, Paul Mellon Fund

title.part: Dickinson, Joseph, approximately 1805-1865. Flora of Liverpool
